title: शिव तांडव स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
जटाटवीगलज्जलप्रवाहपावितस्थले
गलेऽवलम्ब्य लम्बितां भुजंगतुंगमालिकाम्।
डमड्डमड्डमड्डमन्निनादवड्डमर्वयं
चकार चंडतांडवं तनोतु नः शिवः शिवम्॥१॥

जटाकटाहसम्भ्रमभ्रमन्निलिम्पनिर्झरी-
विलोलवीचिवल्लरीविराजमानमूर्धनि।
धगद्धगद्धगज्ज्वलल्ललाटपट्टपावके
किशोरचंद्रशेखरे रतिः प्रतिक्षणं मम॥२॥

धराधरेंद्रनंदिनीविलासबन्धुबन्धुर
स्फुरद्दिगंतसंततिप्रमोदमानमानसे।
कृपाकटाक्षधोरणीनिरुद्धदुर्धरापदि
क्वचिद्विगम्बरे मनो विनोदमेतु वस्तुनि॥३॥

जटाभुजंगपिंगलस्फुरत्फणामणिप्रभा
कदम्बकुंकुमद्रवप्रलिप्तदिग्वधूमुखे।
मदांधसिन्धुरस्फुरत्त्वगुत्तरीयमेदुरे
मनो विनोदमद्भुतं बिभर्तु भूतभर्तरि॥४॥

सहस्रलोचनप्रभृत्यशेषलेखशेखर
प्रसूनधूलिधोरणी विधूसरांघ्रिपीठभूः।
भुजंगराजमालया निबद्धजाटजूटक
श्रियै चिरायजायतां चकोरबंधुशेखरः॥५॥

ललाटचत्वरज्वलद्धनंजयस्फुलिंगभा-
निपीतपंचसायकं नमन्निलिंपनायकम्।
सुधामयूखलेखया विराजमानशेखरं
महाकपालिसम्पदेशिरोजटालमस्तु नः॥६॥
transliteration:
Jatatavigalajjala Pravahapavitasthale
Galevlamby Lambitam Bhujangtunga Malikam.
Damad Damad Damaddama Ninnadavaddamarvayam
Chakara Chandtandavam Tanotu Nah Shivah Shivam.

Jatakataha Sambhramabhramannilimpa Nirjhari
Vilola Vichi Vallari Virajamana Murdhani.
Dhagaddhagad Dhagajjvalal Lalata Pattapavake
Kishora Chandrashekhare Ratih Pratikshanam Mama.

Dharadharendra Nandini Vilasa Bandhubandhura
Sphuraddiganta Santati Pramodamana Manase.
Krupakataksha Dhorani Niruddhadurdharapadi
Kvachid Vigambare Mano Vinodametu Vastuni.

Jata Bhujanga Pingala Sphurat Phanamaniprabha
Kadamba Kumkuma Drava Pralipta Digvadhumukhe.
Madandha Sindhura Sphurat Tvaguttariya Medure
Mano Vinodam Adbhutam Bibhartu Bhutabhartari.

Sahasralochana Prabhritya Sheshalekhashekhara
Prasuna Dhuli Dhorani Vidhusaranghripithabhuh.
Bhujanga Rajamalaya Nibaddha Jatajutaka
Shriyai Chiraya Jayatam Chakora Bandhushekharah.

Lalata Chatvara Jvalad Dhananjaya Sphulingabha
Nipita Panchasayakam Namannilimpanayakam.
Sudhamayukha Lekhaya Virajamana Shekharam
Mahakapa Lisampadeshiroja Talamastu Nah.
hindiMeaning:
जिनकी जटाओं के जंगल से बहती हुई गंगा की धारा से पवित्र स्थल पर, गले में लटकती हुई ऊँची सर्पमाला को लटकाए हुए, डमरू की ध्वनि करते हुए, शिव ने प्रचंड तांडव नृत्य किया। वे शिव हमें कल्याण प्रदान करें।

जटाओं से निकलती गंगा की उत्ताल लहरों वाले मस्तक पर, ललाट पर धधकती अग्नि के साथ, बाल चंद्रमा को धारण करने वाले शिव में मेरी प्रतिक्षण रति हो।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीशिवताण्डवस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य दशमुखरावण ऋषिः। वर्णवृत्त छन्दः। श्रीसदाशिवो देवता। ॐ बीजम्। शिवशक्तिः शक्तिः। श्रीशिवप्रीत्यर्थे शिवताण्डवस्तोत्रजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Pleases Lord Shiva and invokes his blessings
Traditionally recited for removal of fear and grants courage
One of the most powerful Shiva stotras composed by Ravana
Invokes the blessing of spiritual power and divine grace
description: Dedicated to Lord Shiva, the Shiv Tandav Stotram (शिव तांडव स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Composed by Ravana. Regular recitation is believed to bestow spiritual power and divine grace, please Lord Shiva and invoke his blessings, and grant immense devotion and strength. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri. Comprising 6 verses, it can be completed in approximately 10 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Shiv Tandav Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shiv Tandav Stotram in Sanskrit with transliteration, meaning & free PDF. Ravana's hymn to Lord Shiva's cosmic dance.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Shiv Tandav Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shiv Tandav Stotram was composed by Ravana, the king of Lanka and a great devotee of Lord Shiva. According to tradition, Ravana sang the hymn after Shiva pinned him beneath Mount Kailash with his toe — the spontaneous outpouring earned him forgiveness and the divine sword Chandrahasa.

seoFaqs.question: How many verses are in the Shiv Tandav Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shiv Tandav Stotram contains 17 quatrains (some recensions count 16 with the phalashruti separately). It is composed in the rapid Panchachamara meter, mirroring the rhythm of Shiva's cosmic Tandava dance.

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of chanting Shiv Tandav Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditionally, recitation of the Shiv Tandav Stotram is said to invoke Shiva's blessings of strength, fearlessness, mental clarity, and removal of negative energy. The phalashruti at the end of the stotra promises wealth, beauty, and the grace of Shiva for those who recite it with devotion.

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Shiv Tandav Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Shiv Tandav Stotram glorifies Lord Shiva as Nataraja performing the cosmic Tandava — the dance of creation, preservation, and dissolution. Each verse vividly describes Shiva's matted locks bearing the Ganga, the crescent moon, the third eye, the serpent garlands, and the rhythm of the damaru.

seoFaqs.question: When should Shiv Tandav Stotram be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotra is traditionally chanted on Mondays (the day of Shiva), during Pradosham, on Mahashivratri, and during the month of Shravan. It is also recited during Sandhya, especially the evening Pradosh hour when Shiva is said to perform the Tandava.

seoFaqs.question: What is the origin story of Shiv Tandav Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Ravana attempted to lift Mount Kailash to carry Shiva to Lanka. Shiva pressed the mountain down with his toe, crushing Ravana's hands. To appease the Lord, Ravana spontaneously sang this hymn praising Shiva's Tandava dance, after which Shiva forgave him and granted him divine boons.
